summary: Kinnship Build #50 Bug #1 hotfix (Stale SOS Auto-Resume Trap) — BACKEND ONLY. Added 10-test pytest suite covering T1-T8 from the review request in /app/backend/tests/test_build50_hotfix_migration.py. All 10 new tests PASS. Also re-ran the pre-existing test_build50_sos_resolve.py suite (7 tests) — all PASS, including TestSOSLegacyRejected which had failed in iter10 and is now green thanks to `model_config = ConfigDict(extra='forbid')` on SOSRequest (server.py:468). Total: 17/17 passing. Startup migration verified idempotent via live supervisorctl restart — on the 2nd boot the housekeeping/backfill/promote log lines are absent (all only emit when modified_count>0), and there is no Traceback or 'migration skipped' warning. DB spot-check confirms 0 alerts remain without the `resolved` field (59/59 have resolved=false).
critical_code_review_comments: Migration handler _migrate_alerts_backfill_v50 (server.py:3392) correctly uses {'$exists': False} to gate the backfill and {'resolved': {'$in': [False, None]}} to gate legacy-SOS promotion — both make the migration a true no-op on the second boot.

The migration catches exceptions and logs 'alerts backfill migration skipped: {e}' instead of aborting startup — good defensive behaviour, but do note that any future silent skip would still be caught by T1 (we assert that phrase is NOT in the post-restart log tail).
server.py:3427 uses `$created_at` inside an aggregation pipeline `$set` — this is Mongo 4.2+ syntax. Works with the running deployment; worth pinning in requirements docs.
Alert model default `resolved: bool = False` (server.py:362) is what backs T3. Confirmed via DB inspection after a fresh /api/sos call.
Resolve endpoint at server.py:2009 correctly family-scopes the find_one AND update_one — cross-tenant T6 confirms 404 with no doc mutation.
DELETE /api/alerts (server.py:2063) is scoped to caller's family_group_id — safe.

success_rate.backend: 100% (17/17 across both suites; 10/10 for new T1-T8 suite)

seed_data_creation: Each test creates its own disposable SOS alert as Alice (family_group_id=5e214a2f-c794-4649-bbd0-f42fbf2c32da) via POST /api/sos and cleans up via mongo.alerts.delete_one in fixture teardown. T6 inserts one synthetic alert under a random foreign family_group_id and deletes it in a finally block. T8 inserts two TEST_ prefixed seed alerts then calls DELETE /api/alerts which wipes them along with any other alerts Alice's family had. No production/seeded data touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two Build 50 test files now live: (a) test_build50_sos_resolve.py — original 7-test Phase-2 regression, (b) test_build50_hotfix_migration.py — new 10-test T1-T8 suite for the stale-SOS auto-resume trap hotfix. Run both with `pytest backend/tests/test_build50_sos_resolve.py backend/tests/test_build50_hotfix_migration.py -v`. Both use Alice's long-lived JWT (kc_token) from /app/memory/test_credentials.md line 11. TARGET URL: EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL public preview URL (fallback localhost:8001). T1 restarts the backend via `sudo supervisorctl restart backend` and grep-parses /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log for absence of backfill lines after a sentinel marker — will need to be adjusted if supervisor sudo access is removed. All Build-50 SOS endpoints (/api/sos, /api/alerts, /api/alerts/{id}/ack, /api/alerts/{id}/resolve, DELETE /api/alerts) are covered.
rca_of_issue: No open issues. Verification of the hotfix: (1) startup migration ran successfully on boot per backend.err.log tail ('removed 4 alert(s) older than 30 days' + 'set resolved=False on 57 legacy alert(s)'); (2) after a fresh supervisorctl restart, none of the three migration log lines re-appear — confirming true idempotency (the `$exists:False` and `$in:[False,None]` predicates naturally short-circuit on the 2nd run); (3) DB post-migration state: 0 alerts missing `resolved`, all 59 = resolved:false; (4) POST /api/sos legacy `fall_detected` payload correctly rejected with 422 (SOSRequest now has extra='forbid') — this closes the iter10 hanging issue; (5) resolve endpoint family-scoping, 404 semantics, idempotent short-circuit, and DELETE /api/alerts all behave per spec.
